title: uragoga egensis
side_effects: Uragoga egensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, nausea, and vomiting, which may occur due to its high saponin content. Ingestion of large doses can also lead to dizziness, headaches, and even kidney damage in severe cases. Furthermore, the improper use of uragoga egensis may cause allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, itching, and rashes. Additionally, it may interact with certain medications, exacerbating existing health conditions like high blood pressure and diabetes.
